Toss Update: DC won the toss and chose to field! 

LANDMARKS ON THE LINE

 

VENUE AT A GLANCE
Ekana Stadium has leaned heavily towards chasing sides in recent times. Across the 15 IPL matches played here since 2024, teams batting second have dominated with 10 wins, while those setting a target have managed just five victories. The average first innings score is a formidable 189, with powerplays producing around 57 runs. Death overs have offered little relief to bowlers, who have conceded at 10.5 runs an over. Pace bowlers have enjoyed greater success, accounting for 70 percent of the wickets, while spinners have chipped in with the remaining 30 percent.



HEAD TO HEAD IN IPL
Delhi Capitals hold a narrow advantage over Lucknow Super Giants in their IPL rivalry, edging ahead with four wins compared to LSG’s three across seven encounters. With such fine margins separating the two sides, this matchup remains delicately balanced and primed for another closely fought contest.

 

PLAYER BATTLES TO WATCH OUT FOR 


Fiery versus clever, this is a showdown built for prime-time drama. Rishabh Pant’s instinctive, no fear approach clashes head on with Kuldeep Yadav’s deceptive left arm wizardry, a duel that’s swung on subtle margins. Across five innings, Pant scores at a strike rate of 92, but Kuldeep has had the last laugh three times, slipping past the bat with variation and dip. Then comes the classic contest of poise against precision. KL Rahul’s silken timing and control power a strike rate of 122.85 in five innings, yet Mohammed Shami’s relentless back of a length accuracy and seam movement have accounted for him twice. These aren’t just numbers, they’re pressure points. Two battles, four elite operators, and match defining moments ready to explode.

 

 

PROBABLE PLAYING XI :

LUCKNOW SUPER GIANTS : A Markram, M Marsh, N Pooran, R Pant (C/Wk), Breetzke, A Badoni, A Samad, S Ahmed. M Khan, A Khan, M Shami , Digvesh Rathi (Impact Sub)

 

DELHI CAPITALS : KL Rahul (Wk), A Porel / P Nissanka , N Rana, A Patel (C), T Stubbs, D Miller , A Sharma , V Nigam, K Yadav, L Ngidi, M Kumar, T Natarajan (Impact Sub)

 

STRATEGIC TRUMPCARDS TO WATCH OUT 

Ashutosh Sharma headlines the X factor narrative with ice cold finishing pedigree and proven big moment intent. The right hander stamped authority last season against this very opposition, sealing the game in the final over with clean hitting and fearless shot selection. His numbers underline the threat, 393 runs in 24 matches at a blistering strike rate of 163.75, including a career best 66*. Competing against him is Digvesh Rathi, the unconventional leg spinner who bends norms and conversations alike. In just 13 matches, Rathi has picked up 14 wickets at a strike rate of 22.29, maintaining pressure with an economy of 8.25. Flashy celebrations aside, his impact is real, one closer, one disruptor, both ready to tilt momentum.

 


Lucknow is locked in and loud as Match 05 of TATA IPL 2026 brings back a spicy rivalry under the Ekana lights. Lucknow Super Giants take on Delhi Capitals in a clash that already has history written all over it. For context, rewind to IPL 2025’s opening clash for these two sides, a certified humdinger where Delhi snatched victory from the jaws of defeat, thanks to Ashutosh Sharma’s ice cold last over finish and some clutch support from Vipraj Nigam. That game still lives rent free in fans’ heads, and tonight feels like unfinished business.

LSG are pulling up stacked, especially in the pace department. The return of Mohammed Shami brings old school control and big game experience, while left arm angle merchant Mohsin Khan adds variety. And then there’s Mayank Yadav, the human speedgun. If Ekana offers its usual two paced bounce early on, this trio could straight up make life uncomfortable for Delhi’s top order.But don’t sleep on the Capitals. The big headline  David Miller in Delhi colours, turning up against his former franchise. The man’s clutch gene is elite, and in pressure chases, he’s still him. Pair that with Delhi’s fearless middle order, and you’ve got a unit that thrives when chaos hits.

Ekana usually rewards discipline and smart match ups, so expect captains to play chess, not checkers. With emotion, history, and serious firepower on both sides, this one’s giving high voltage vibes. Fans, lock in, this could be cinema again.
MatchSummary.PostMatchCommentary:
Sameer Rizvi (Player of the Match):
"I was told clearly during practice that I would bat at number four and the coaches gave me a lot of backing which helped my confidence. Playing at home made a difference because I know these conditions well from my state league cricket. I wanted to be positive early but once two wickets fell the situation changed and Stubbs reminded me to respect the conditions first. After getting set we knew we could play our natural game without rushing. It was never about reaching a fifty but about taking on the bad balls and putting them away when the opportunity came."

Clutch. Impact. Sam. ???????? pic.twitter.com/mCkNgeuhYm

— Delhi Capitals (@DelhiCapitals) April 1, 2026


Axar Patel (DC Captain):
"The game finished early but we came close in the middle and the conversation with the bat was always about building one big partnership before finishing strongly. With the new ball doing a bit we were very happy with how our bowlers responded and executed the plans we discussed. Knowing the pitch was helping the pacers and with a big boundary I felt it made sense to use them more and bowl myself less. The plan was to introduce Rizvi early while keeping Ashutosh Sharma as an option if the innings started well. Ashutosh showed last year what he can do and this time Rizvi stepped up, he has been excellent in practice and it was good to see him pull it off tonight."


Rishabh Pant (LSG Captain):
"Ideal way to move on from a run out is to ignore it because dwelling on it only puts you on the back foot and there is nothing you can change once it has happened. With limited bowling options we were forced into using a batter because we could not build partnerships or put enough runs on a good surface. The batting order call was about left right combinations and giving Badoni responsibility in tough situations even though it did not come off this time. There was some help with the new ball but with only one hundred and forty runs it is hard to put the opposition under pressure despite the boys fighting hard. Extra runs always hurt and for the rest of the season you will definitely see me continue batting in the top order even if it remains a fifty fifty call."


Delhi Capitals scripted a calm and controlled chase at the Ekana Stadium, overcoming early damage to defeat Lucknow Super Giants by six wickets with 17 balls to spare.

Lucknow were bundled out for 141 in 18.4 overs after never quite settling with the bat. Mitchell Marsh top scored with 35 from 28 balls, while Abdul Samad added late resistance with 36 off 25, but wickets fell at regular intervals. Lucknow slipped from 65 for 4 in the ninth over to 105 for 6 by the thirteenth, losing momentum completely. Delhi’s bowling unit worked in tandem, led by T Natarajan who claimed 3 for 29, while Kuldeep Yadav and Lungi Ngidi chipped in with five wickets between them to keep the scoring rate under eight an over.

The chase appeared shaky at first as Delhi slumped to 26 for 4 inside five overs, including a first ball dismissal for KL Rahul. From there, Sameer Rizvi assembled a match defining partnership with Tristan Stubbs, adding an unbroken 119 for the fifth wicket. Rizvi anchored the innings with a composed 70 off 47 balls, striking at nearly 149, while Stubbs supported with 39 from 32.

Delhi reached 145 for 4 in 17.1 overs, sealing a victory built on recovery, patience and a decisive middle order stand that swung the game firmly in their favour.
